archetype: Vibrant Play (Archetype 6) with Neo-brutalist tactile elements

headings.usage: Use for all main headers, lesson titles, and gamified celebration text.

body.usage: Use for all lesson content, descriptions, and UI labels.

code.usage: Use for all code-checking exercises and programming theory cards.

HUD_Header.description: Sticky top bar showing current track, streak flame, gems, and hearts.
HUD_Header.layout: flex justify-between items-center px-4 py-3 bg-white border-b-2 border-slate-200 sticky top-0 z-50

Skill_Tree_Node.description: Circular button on the learning path. Staggered layout using margin-left/right to create a winding snake path.
Skill_Tree_Node.styles: w-16 h-16 sm:w-20 sm:h-20 rounded-full flex items-center justify-center text-3xl border-b-[6px] active:border-b-0 active:translate-y-[6px] transition-all

Multiple_Choice_Card.styles: p-4 border-2 rounded-2xl cursor-pointer transition-all flex items-center gap-4 text-lg font-medium

Code_Block_Card.description: Draggable or clickable code fragments for 'arrange the code' exercises.
Code_Block_Card.styles: px-4 py-3 bg-slate-800 text-green-400 font-mono text-sm sm:text-base rounded-xl border-b-4 border-slate-900 cursor-pointer shadow-sm hover:translate-y-[-2px] transition-transform

Bottom_Action_Bar.description: Fixed footer in lessons containing the 'Check' or 'Continue' button.
Bottom_Action_Bar.styles: fixed bottom-0 left-0 w-full p-4 sm:p-6 bg-white border-t-2 border-slate-200 flex items-center justify-between z-50
Bottom_Action_Bar.button: w-full sm:w-auto min-w-[200px] py-3 text-lg font-bold uppercase tracking-wide text-center text-white bg-[#58CC02] border-b-[6px] border-[#58CC02]/80 rounded-xl hover:bg-[#46A302] active:border-b-0 active:translate-y-[6px]

Celebration_Screen.description: Full screen overlay when lesson is complete.
Celebration_Screen.styles: fixed inset-0 bg-yellow-400 flex flex-col items-center justify-center text-center z-[100] p-6
Celebration_Screen.stats_card: bg-white p-6 rounded-2xl border-b-[6px] border-yellow-500 w-full max-w-sm flex flex-col gap-4 text-yellow-600 font-bold text-xl

motion_and_interactions.buttons: All primary actions must use the tactile border-b trick. They physically depress when clicked.
motion_and_interactions.progress_bar: Width transition must be smooth (transition-all duration-500 ease-out).
motion_and_interactions.feedback_pop: When a user selects an answer, the card should slightly scale up (scale-105) for 150ms.
motion_and_interactions.celebration: Use framer-motion or confetti to celebrate lesson completion.

media_and_icons.instructions: Use lucide-react for UI icons (Heart, Flame, Gem, Star, Lock, Check, X). Icons in HUD must be filled/colored.

testing.attributes: All interactive elements (answers, continue buttons, track switches) MUST have data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='answer-choice-1', data-testid='check-answer-button', data-testid='lesson-node-2').

instructions_to_main_agent: Install Google Fonts 'Fredoka' and 'Nunito' via index.css import or link tags.

Do NOT use standard flat buttons. Implement the 'tactile button' CSS trick using tailwind classes (border-b-[length] + active:translate-y-[length] + active:border-b-0) to create a physically pressable feel. Apply this to lesson nodes and check buttons.
For the lesson path (tree), use absolute positioning or flex layouts with offset margins to create a winding trail.
Build the multiple choice quiz component to have visual states for default, selected, correct (green), and incorrect (red).
Build a code-block arrangement exercise where code segments look like dark-mode tactile cards.
Include a sticky gamification header showing Hearts, Gems, and Streak Flame across all main screens.
Do NOT add authentication. Assume a single local user profile.
